mechanism_explained: The IDK-IDK mechanism: engagement metrics decouple from learning when the chatbot becomes the relationship
Complexity threshold test: three conditions (adaptive sequencing / live instrumentation / multi-layered capability) determine when full architecture is warranted
Butter Knife Fallacy: judging the scalpel's potential by observing its average misuse; the Bastani GPT Base vs Tutor finding shows the guardrail design is the variable
Bolt-on failure: seven signals require integration the assembled-tools approach cannot produce; the layers are a loop not a stack
Decoupling problem: the artifact is no longer evidence of the process; seven GLP components reconstruct the friction signature that genuine learning leaves
Phase gates as measurement decisions: each mode is gated on a specific GLP signal so the mode arrives when the learner is ready for it
Within-learner contextual bandit: Thompson sampling over (mode × concept × time) cells with GLP-based reward; cross-learner generalization is outside the engine's scope by design
Backward design as the curriculum instrument: outcomes first, assessment second, content structure third — the concept map the engine needs cannot emerge from topic coverage
Cost-collapse asymmetry: AI content at marginal cost lowers burden of proof for low-risk content while leaving it unchanged for high-risk content
Binding-cost shift: when production cost collapses the binding constraint shifts to knowledge of the individual learner; cheap content makes PCK more valuable not less
Eight open questions as instrumented research bets: the platform is the experimental apparatus the questions are designed to answer
The map requires the thinking: Medhavy the instrument amplifies the design conversation the human conducts; the tool cannot replace it

The wrapper-is-the-variable principle — same GPT-4 model, prompt-level guardrail alone produced the Bastani 17-point gap, making architectural commitments the unit of analysis rather than the underlying model
The three-question sufficiency test (scale / measurement-need / willingness-to-act) as the load-bearing institutional diagnostic — any 'no' sends the reader to the simpler Kindle-only stack

Three commitments stack — RAG grounding + hints-not-answers + calibrated assistance — implementing the generation effect in software while staying inside the Koedinger-Aleven inverted-U of the assistance dilemma

Four Quiz Me differentiators over Anki (concept-map integration, priority weighting, aviation override, prerequisite interleaving) plus the habit-loop institutional-commitment requirement that makes Question 3 concrete
The assistance dilemma — why graduated hints solve the offload/shutdown trade-off at scale (Koedinger & Aleven 2007 with Hattie-Timperley feedback layer)
Same engagement metrics, opposite learning outcomes — the wrapper is the variable; Canvas measures administration, not learning
Prediction-error encoding — why bypassing the prediction (via borrowed AI certainty) bypasses the encoding signal that produces durable learning
The choice of reward signal — delayed retrieval at seven days vs immediate accuracy — is what makes Medhavy's loop architecturally different from existing adaptive systems
The Q3 willingness-to-act condition decomposed into three concrete subcomponents (named owner, budgeted time, political precommitment) — what 'willing' actually requires institutionally
The LTI handshake reduced to 'Canvas vouches, Medhavy trusts the vouch, grades flow back the same way' — technical setup measured in days, institutional approval in months
The six-field hybrid decision tool with side-by-side AI+1-only vs +Medhavy comparison table, plus a three-sentences-plus-one-question pitch structure for budget committees
